title: theme — data classification
description: Theme rows carry NO PII beyond created_by / updated_by references — the tokens themselves are design values. user_theme_preferences carries per-user preferences (mode + accessibility flags) which are functional metadata, not personal identifiers.

audit_log: Every ThemeActivated / ThemeDeactivated / ThemeTokenOverridden / ThemeAccessibilityFlagChanged event dual-written to activity + audit lanes.

user_theme_preferences updates fire UserThemePreferenceUpdated which activity captures — no audit trail for the user-scoped row (design decision: per-user preference is functional, not compliance-relevant).

notes: Theme rows are public within the tenant boundary — every authenticated tenant user with theme.read can inspect the rows.
Cross-tenant reads return zero rows via BelongsToTenant global scope — platform presets (tenant_id NULL) are the exception and are cross-tenant PUBLIC readable by design.
tokens_snapshot is the SAME class as the underlying tokens — it's a materialised view for immutability, not a security boundary.
user_theme_preferences (in user module) is 'internal' by default; the row is per-user and never surfaces cross-user.
